summary.context: Java OrderProcessor with a single 80+ line method handling order routing, tax/shipping calculation, currency conversion, and database persistence.

findings.title: Hardcoded database credentials
findings.issue: Database credentials ('root', 'password123') hardcoded in source code
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager

findings.title: Deeply nested conditional structure (5+ levels)
findings.issue: 5+ levels of nesting makes code hard to read, test, and modify
findings.suggestion: Use early returns (guard clauses) to flatten the structure

findings.title: Magic numbers throughout the code
findings.issue: Unnamed constants (0.08, 5.99, 100, 10000, 0.85, 0.73, 110.0) are hard to understand and dangerous to change
findings.suggestion: Extract to named constants (TAX_RATE, STANDARD_SHIPPING, etc.)

findings.title: Duplicated currency conversion and database code
findings.issue: Currency conversion and database insertion logic duplicated between branches
findings.suggestion: Extract into shared private methods: convertCurrency(), saveOrder()

findings.title: Swallowed exceptions with generic error return
findings.issue: Catches broad Exception, returns generic 'ERROR' with no logging
findings.suggestion: Catch specific exceptions, log with stack trace, return descriptive error

findings.title: Method is too long (80+ lines)
findings.issue: Single method handles routing, calculation, conversion, and persistence
findings.suggestion: Extract into focused methods: calculateTax(), calculateShipping(), convertCurrency(), saveOrder()
